POS; REPLY MEMO AND RESPONSE TO PLTF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION FOR INCONVENIENT FORUM FILED BY DEFENDANT TANG, SUNG CHING

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUNG TANG MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY THE ACTION FOR INCONVENIENT FORUM. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED, THIS ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF LITIGATION IN HONG KONG. HONG KONG IS A SUITABLE ALTERNATIVE FORUM. DEFENDANT IS A HONG KONG RESIDENT AND IS AMENABLE TO JURISDICTION THERE. THE BALANCE OF PUBLIC AND PRIVATE INTEREST FACTORS MAKES IT JUST FOR LITIGATION TO PROCEED IN HONG KONG. TRIAL AND ENFORCEMENT OF THE JUDGMENT WILL BE LESS EXPENSIVE AND MORE EXPEDITIOUS IN HONG KONG GIVEN THAT MOST OF THE KEY WITNESSES RESIDE THERE. CALIFORNIA DOES NOT HAVE A SIGNIFICANT INTEREST IN THIS LITIGATION. THE TWO CORPORATIONS INCORPORATED IN CALIFORNIA ARE BOTH SUBSIDIARIES OF PARENT CORPORATIONS INCORPORATED OUT OF THE COUNTRY. A RELATED SUIT IS PENDING IN HONG KONG. NO SHOWING THAT HONG KONG WOULD BE AN INCONVENIENT FORUM. THE COURT ADDS: DEFINITION OF "PROPERTY" FOR QUIET TITLE INCLUDES REAL AND PERSONAL PROPERTY. THE COURT ALSO, RULES THAT FOLLOWING THE APPROPRIATE PAPERWORK TO THE COURT, DEFAULT JUDGMENTS MAY BE ENTERED AS TO UNKNOWN DEFENDANTS ONLY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Or Stay The Action For Inconvenient Forum And Req. For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Jan-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SUNG TANG Motion to Dismiss or Stay the Action for Inconvenient Forum. Appearances: Chinin Tana, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 788-8811 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Simone M. Katz-O'Neill, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (415) 421-6500, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted, this action is stayed pending the completion of litigation in Hong Kong. Hong Kong is a suitable alternative forum. Defendant is a Hong Kong resident and is amenable to jurisdiction there. The balance of public and private interest factors makes it just for litigation to proceed in Hong Kong. Trial and enforcement of the judgment will be less expensive and more expeditious in Hong Kong given that most of the key witnesses reside there. California does not have a significant interest in this litigation. The two corporations incorporated in California are both subsidiaries of parent corporations incorporated out of the country. A related suit is pending in Hong Kong. No showing that Hong Kong would be an inconvenient forum. The Court adds: Definition of "property" for quiet title includes real and personal property. The Court also, rules that following the appropriate paperwork to the Court, default judgments may be entered as to unknown defendants only. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Amended Notice Ofmotino To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Mar-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SUNG TANG'S AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION.
APPEARANCES: CHININ TANA, ESQUIRE (415.788.8811) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ERICK HOWARD, ESQUIRE (SHARTSIS FRIESE, 415.421.6500) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE COMPLAINT REFLECTS THAT DEFENDANT IS A HONG KONG RESIDENT AND PLAINTIFF'S ONLY CLAIM IS THAT DEFENDANT CLAIMS AN INTEREST IN A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION AND HAS FILED A DECLARATION IN ANOTHER CALIFORNIA CASE. NEITHER THE CLAIM OF OWNERSHIP NOR THE LIMITED CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA PROVIDE A CONSTITUTIONAL BASIS FOR JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE RULED UPON AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULE; REQUEST TO STRIKE THE DECLARATION OF MR. HOWARD PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1005(B) AND REQUEST TO STRIKE EXHIBIT A OF THE HOWARD DECLARATION. SUSTAIN THE AUTHENTICATION OBJECTIONS. REGARDING SUNG CHIN TANG'S DECLARATION: OVERRULE OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPHS ONE, THREE AND FOUR. PARAGRAPH FIVE: STRIKE THE LAST TWO SENTENCES. PARAGRAPH 6: STRIKE LAST SENTENCE. REGARDING YENG WAI CHUNG'S DECLARATION; COURT DENIES THE REQUEST TO STRIKE THE ENTIRE DECLARATION. OVERRULE OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPHS TWO AND THREE. IN PARAGRAPH FIVE; STRIKE BEGINNING AT THE WORD "THOUGH" AND ENDING WITH THE WORD "PERMIT". MR. HOWARD TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Deft Sung Ching Tang For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
